tools.name: obsidian_get_note
tools.title: Obsidian Get Note
tools.description: Read a note from the vault — by path, the active file, or a periodic note. Choose a `format` projection: raw body, full object, structural document map, or a single section.

format.description: Which projection to return. `content` — raw markdown body. `full` — content plus parsed frontmatter, tags, and file metadata. `document-map` — catalog of headings, block IDs, and frontmatter field names (use to discover patch targets). `section` — a single heading, block, or frontmatter section (requires `section`); heading sections include the full subtree under that heading and use `Parent::Child` syntax for nesting.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

section.description: Required when `format` is `"section"`. Identifies the heading/block/frontmatter to extract.

type.description: Heading by name, block by reference, or frontmatter field by key.

target.description: Heading name (use "::" for nested headings), block reference without leading caret (e.g. "2d9b4a", not "^2d9b4a"), or frontmatter field name.

includeLinks.description: When true with `format: "full"`, parses outgoing wiki and markdown link references from the note body. Skipped for other formats.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

oneOf.description: Content-only projection.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

frontmatter.description: Parsed YAML frontmatter. Values are strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, or nested objects.

tags.description: Tags from frontmatter and inline #tag syntax.

outgoingLinks.description: Outgoing link references parsed from the note body. Present when `includeLinks` is true. Vault-internal references only — external URLs (http, mailto, etc.) are filtered out.

target.description: Link target as written — vault path, basename, or alias. No existence check.

oneOf.description: Full projection — content plus parsed metadata.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

oneOf.description: Document-map projection — catalog of patch targets.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

type.description: Heading by name, block by reference, or frontmatter field by key.

target.description: Heading name (use "::" for nested headings), block reference without leading caret (e.g. "2d9b4a", not "^2d9b4a"), or frontmatter field name.

valueText.description: Section value as raw markdown (heading/block sections).

valueJson.description: Section value as the JSON-typed frontmatter value (frontmatter sections only).

oneOf.description: Single-section projection.

result.description: Mode-discriminated projection of the requested note.

tools.name: obsidian_list_notes
tools.title: Obsidian List Notes
tools.description: List notes and subdirectories at a vault path. Defaults to the vault root when `path` is omitted. Tune recursion with `depth`, or filter the walk with `extension` / `nameRegex`. Capped at 1000 entries per call — when reached, walking stops and `excluded` is set; narrow `path` or tighten filters to surface the rest.

path.description: Vault-relative directory path. Omit for the vault root.

extension.description: Only include files matching this extension, with or without leading dot. Applies to files only — directories are returned regardless.

nameRegex.description: Optional ECMAScript regex (no flags) applied to entry names. Matches both files and directories; directories that fail the regex are skipped without recursing into them.

depth.description: How many directory levels to walk. `1` = target directory only (no recursion); `2` = target plus its immediate children — a structural overview; bump higher to drill in. Prefer narrowing `path` to a subdirectory over a high `depth` on the vault root.

path.description: The directory listed (empty string for vault root).

type.description: Whether this entry is a regular file or a subdirectory.

truncated.description: On directory entries: true when the depth limit prevented walking into this directory. Pass a deeper `depth` to expand.

items.description: A single entry in the listing.

entries.description: Entries in DFS order — top-level first, then descendants.
Total entries returned across all walked depths.

extension.description: Extension filter applied, normalized with leading dot.

nameRegex.description: nameRegex filter applied to this listing.

depth.description: Recursion depth used to produce this listing.

appliedFilters.description: Active filters and the recursion depth that produced this listing.

excluded.description: Present when the walk was truncated by the global entry cap.

reason.description: `entry_cap`: walk stopped because the per-call entry limit was reached.

cap.description: The cap value at which walking stopped.

tools.name: obsidian_list_tags
tools.title: Obsidian List Tags
tools.description: List every tag found across the vault, with usage counts. Includes hierarchical parents — `work/tasks` contributes to both `work` and `work/tasks`. To find notes by tag, use `obsidian_search_notes` in dataview mode (e.g. `TABLE FROM #work`).

items.description: A tag with its usage count.

tags.description: All tags in the vault, in upstream-provided order.

tools.name: obsidian_search_notes
tools.title: Obsidian Search Notes
tools.description: Search the vault by text substring, Dataview DQL query, or JSONLogic predicate. Pick the mode that matches the query shape. Results cap at 100 hits with an `excluded` indicator when more were available; text-mode hits also clip per file at `maxMatchesPerHit`. On large vaults, narrow with `pathPrefix` or lower `maxMatchesPerHit` to keep responses compact.

mode.description: Which search algorithm to run. `text` matches a substring case-insensitively across filenames and note bodies, returning surrounding context windows. `dataview` runs a DQL query (e.g. `TABLE WHERE file.mtime > date(today)`) for path/date/metadata filters. `jsonlogic` evaluates a JSONLogic tree against each note, with `var` paths into `path`, `content`, `frontmatter.<key>`, `tags`, and `stat.{ctime,mtime,size}`, plus `glob` and `regexp` operators.

query.description: For `text` mode: substring to match. For `dataview` mode: the DQL query string. Required for those modes.

logic.description: JSONLogic tree. Required and only used when `mode` is `"jsonlogic"`.

contextLength.description: Characters of context on each side of the match (text mode only).

pathPrefix.description: Filter returned filenames by prefix (text mode only, applied client-side).

maxMatchesPerHit.description: Cap on match contexts returned per file in text mode. When clipped, the hit carries `truncated: true` and `totalMatches`.

filename.description: Vault-relative path of the matching note.

score.description: Relevance score from the search index, when available.

start.description: Match start offset in the surrounding context.

end.description: Match end offset in the surrounding context.

match.description: Match offsets within the context window.

items.description: A single match within a file.

matches.description: Per-match context windows. Capped per file by `maxMatchesPerHit`.

totalMatches.description: Total matches in this file. Present only when `matches` was clipped to `maxMatchesPerHit`.

truncated.description: True when `matches` was clipped to `maxMatchesPerHit`. Use `obsidian_get_note` to read the full file when more context is needed.

items.description: A file with one or more text-search matches.

excluded.description: Present when results were truncated to the cap.

count.description: Number of additional hits the upstream returned beyond the cap.

oneOf.description: Text-search results.

filename.description: Vault-relative path of the matching note.

result.description: The query result for this file — shape determined by the query.

items.description: A file with a structured (Dataview/JSONLogic) result value.

hits.description: Matching files with the query result per file.

excluded.description: Present when results were truncated to the cap.

count.description: Number of additional hits the upstream returned beyond the cap.

oneOf.description: Dataview DQL results.

filename.description: Vault-relative path of the matching note.

result.description: The query result for this file — shape determined by the query.

items.description: A file with a structured (Dataview/JSONLogic) result value.

hits.description: Matching files with the JSONLogic result per file.

excluded.description: Present when results were truncated to the cap.

count.description: Number of additional hits the upstream returned beyond the cap.

oneOf.description: JSONLogic results.

tools.name: obsidian_open_in_ui
tools.title: Obsidian Open In Ui
tools.description: Open a file in the Obsidian app UI. By default fails when the path does not exist; the `failIfMissing` flag controls the open-or-create behavior.

path.description: Vault-relative path of the file to open.

failIfMissing.description: When true (default), fails if the file does not exist. When false, allows Obsidian to create the file on open.

newLeaf.description: Open in a new leaf (split pane) instead of the active one.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path that was opened.

opened.description: True when the open call succeeded.

createdIfMissing.description: True when the file did not exist before the call and was created by Obsidian.

tools.name: obsidian_write_note
tools.title: Obsidian Write Note
tools.description: Create or overwrite a note. With `section`, replaces just that heading/block/frontmatter section in place; nested headings need `Parent::Child` syntax — use `obsidian_get_note` with `format: "document-map"` to discover available targets. Whole-file writes fail with `file_exists` against an existing note unless `overwrite: true` — for in-place edits, prefer `obsidian_patch_note` (sections), `obsidian_append_to_note` (append), or `obsidian_replace_in_note` (find-and-replace). For heading sections, `content` is the new body; the heading line is preserved automatically.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

content.description: Body to write. For heading sections, the new section body — do not repeat the heading line (it stays in place). Markdown unless `contentType` is `json`.

section.description: Optional sub-document target. When set, only this section is replaced; rest of the note is untouched.

type.description: Heading by name, block by reference, or frontmatter field by key.

target.description: Heading name (use "::" for nested headings), block reference without leading caret (e.g. "2d9b4a", not "^2d9b4a"), or frontmatter field name.

contentType.description: Content body format. Use "json" for typed frontmatter values or block-targeted table rows. JSON values must be valid JSON literals — strings need quoting (`"\"draft\""`, not `"draft"`), and numbers/booleans/arrays/objects pass through as-is.

overwrite.description: Whole-file mode only (ignored when `section` is set). When `false` (default), the call fails with `file_exists` if the target note already exists — read it first and use `obsidian_patch_note` / `obsidian_append_to_note` / `obsidian_replace_in_note` for in-place edits, or retry with `overwrite: true` for a deliberate full replacement.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note that was written.

sectionTargeted.description: True when only a section was replaced; false for full-file writes.

created.description: True when the write created a new file. False when it replaced an existing one or targeted a section.

tools.name: obsidian_append_to_note
tools.title: Obsidian Append To Note
tools.description: Append content to a note. Without `section`, the body is appended to the end of the file. With `section`, the content is appended to the end of that heading/block/frontmatter; nested headings need `Parent::Child` syntax — use `obsidian_get_note` with `format: "document-map"` to discover available targets. For block-reference targets, content is concatenated adjacent to the block line without inserting a separator — include a leading newline in `content` if you want one. Set `createTargetIfMissing` to bring the target section into existence rather than failing when it does not exist.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

content.description: Body to append. Markdown unless `contentType` is `json`.

section.description: Optional sub-document target. When set, content is appended to that section instead of the file.

type.description: Heading by name, block by reference, or frontmatter field by key.

target.description: Heading name (use "::" for nested headings), block reference without leading caret (e.g. "2d9b4a", not "^2d9b4a"), or frontmatter field name.

contentType.description: Content body format. Use "json" for typed frontmatter values or block-targeted table rows. JSON values must be valid JSON literals — strings need quoting (`"\"draft\""`, not `"draft"`), and numbers/booleans/arrays/objects pass through as-is.

createTargetIfMissing.description: When `section` is provided, create the section if it does not already exist (otherwise the call fails when the section is missing).

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

sectionTargeted.description: True when the append went to a section; false for whole-file appends.

tools.name: obsidian_patch_note
tools.title: Obsidian Patch Note
tools.description: Edit a heading, block reference, or frontmatter field in place — append to, prepend to, or replace the target's body. Use `obsidian_get_note` with `format: "document-map"` to discover available targets first; nested headings need `Parent::Child` syntax.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

type.description: Heading by name, block by reference, or frontmatter field by key.

target.description: Heading name (use "::" for nested headings), block reference without leading caret (e.g. "2d9b4a", not "^2d9b4a"), or frontmatter field name.

operation.description: How to apply `content` relative to the targeted section. `append` — at the end of the target's body (for headings, before the next sibling/parent heading; for frontmatter array fields, as a new array item). `prepend` — at the start. `replace` — swaps the target's body.

content.description: Body to insert/replace. Markdown unless `contentType` is `json`.

contentType.description: Content body format. Use "json" for typed frontmatter values or block-targeted table rows. JSON values must be valid JSON literals — strings need quoting (`"\"draft\""`, not `"draft"`), and numbers/booleans/arrays/objects pass through as-is.

createTargetIfMissing.description: Create the target heading/block/frontmatter field if it does not exist.

applyIfContentPreexists.description: Apply the patch even if matching content already exists in the target. Guards against double-applying.

trimTargetWhitespace.description: Trim whitespace from the target section before applying the operation.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

type.description: Heading by name, block by reference, or frontmatter field by key.

target.description: Heading name (use "::" for nested headings), block reference without leading caret (e.g. "2d9b4a", not "^2d9b4a"), or frontmatter field name.

tools.name: obsidian_replace_in_note
tools.title: Obsidian Replace In Note
tools.description: Search and replace inside a single note, literally or by regex. Replacements run in array order, each over the previous one's output. Use for edits that don't fit `obsidian_patch_note`'s structural targets — e.g., body-wide find-and-replace.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

search.description: Substring or regex pattern to match.

replace.description: Replacement text. Empty string deletes matches.

useRegex.description: Treat `search` as an ECMAScript regex pattern.

wholeWord.description: Match only at word boundaries. Applies in both literal and regex modes — the search pattern is wrapped with `\b…\b`.

flexibleWhitespace.description: Treat any run of whitespace in `search` as matching any whitespace in the body. Literal mode only — has no effect when `useRegex: true` (express it directly with `\s+`).

replaceAll.description: When false, only the first match is replaced.

replacements.description: Replacements to apply in array order over the evolving content.

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the note.

totalReplacements.description: Total number of substitutions applied across all replacement entries.

count.description: Number of matches replaced for this entry.

perReplacement.description: Per-entry counts in the order replacements were applied.

tools.name: obsidian_manage_frontmatter
tools.title: Obsidian Manage Frontmatter
tools.description: Get, set, or delete a single frontmatter key on a note, atomically. `set` requires a JSON-typed `value` (string, number, boolean, array, or object).

operation.description: Operation to perform on `key`. `get` — read the current value. `set` — write `value`, creating the key if absent. `delete` — remove the key from frontmatter.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

value.description: Required when `operation` is `"set"`. JSON-typed value to write — strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, and objects all accepted.

exists.description: True when the key was present in the frontmatter.

value.description: Current value, or `null` when the key is absent.

oneOf.description: Result for `get`.
Result for `set`.
Result for `delete`.

tools.name: obsidian_manage_tags
tools.title: Obsidian Manage Tags
tools.description: Add, remove, or list a note's tags. The server reconciles both representations — frontmatter `tags:` array and inline `#tag` syntax — so an `add` ensures the tag is present in the requested location(s), and a `remove` strips it. Inline `#tag` occurrences inside fenced code blocks are intentionally left alone. Inline-location additions append the new tag at the end of the file. `list` ignores the input `tags` array.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

operation.description: `add` and `remove` mutate the note; `list` reads the current tag set.

tags.description: Tags to add or remove. Omit the leading `#`. Required for add/remove.

location.description: Where to apply the change. Default `both` reconciles both representations.

all.description: Deduplicated union of frontmatter and inline tags.

tags.description: Tags split by location plus the deduplicated union.

oneOf.description: Result for `list`.

applied.description: Tags actually changed by this call.

skipped.description: Tags already present at the targeted location(s).

tags.description: All tags on the note after the change.

oneOf.description: Result for `add`.

applied.description: Tags actually changed by this call.

skipped.description: Tags absent from the targeted location(s).

tags.description: All tags on the note after the change.

oneOf.description: Result for `remove`.

tools.name: obsidian_delete_note
tools.title: Obsidian Delete Note
tools.description: Permanently delete a note from the vault. Confirms with the user before deleting when the client supports interactive confirmation. Recovery requires the local trash in Obsidian — there is no API-level undo.

oneOf.description: Address a note by its vault-relative path.

type.description: Address the file currently open in Obsidian.

oneOf.description: Address whichever file is currently active in the Obsidian UI.

date.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Omit for the current period.

oneOf.description: Address a periodic note (current or dated).

path.description: Resolved vault-relative path of the deleted note.

deleted.description: True when the file was removed.
